meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build SaaS pricing models to overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no project ideas' paralysis requires shifting focus from product to business mechanics. By building a SaaS pricing calculator, you learn the fundamental logic of recurring revenue models. This project bridges the gap between simple coding tasks and enterprise-grade software architecture, giving you a tangible, resume-ready portfolio asset.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the student's greatest adversary. You stare at the blinking cursor, paralyzed by the sheer noise of 'build an AI startup' advice, lacking a concrete starting point. The weight of expectations kills innovation before a single line of code is written.

But here is the secret: you don't need a revolutionary idea to start. You need a functional model. By building a SaaS pricing engine, you move from 'student' to 'systems architect.' We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn a dry business concept into a sophisticated, interactive application.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Logic
steps.description: Establish the base architecture for a three-tier pricing model (Free, Pro, Enterprise). Focus on data structures that handle usage-based billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust data schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers. Include fields for monthly cost, user limits, and feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate pricing schema in TypeScript'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Calculator
steps.description: Create a frontend slider or input system that calculates annual vs. monthly savings for the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React component that acts as a pricing calculator, toggling between monthly and yearly billing cycles with a 20% discount logic.

steps.title: Implement Stripe-Ready Logic
steps.description: Structure your code to interface with payment gateways, ensuring your pricing logic is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing model to output a JSON object compatible with the Stripe Product API, including price IDs and metadata.

steps.title: Add Dynamic Feature Comparison
steps.description: Build a matrix that updates based on the selected tier to demonstrate feature availability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dynamic comparison table component that highlights features based on the state of the selected pricing tier.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Finalize your build and push to a live environment to test your logic in a browser.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates the pricing logic for edge cases, such as zero-value inputs or negative user counts.

pro_tips: Always build your pricing logic in a separate module from the UI to make it testable via unit tests.
Use 'dry-run' flags in your CLI tools to see the code structure before injecting it into your codebase.
Focus on 'Usage-Based' pricing models to differentiate your project from basic flat-fee clones.
Include a 'hidden' enterprise tier that triggers a lead-capture form instead of a checkout button.
Document your decision-making process in a README.md; employers value the 'why' behind your code as much as the code itself.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing calculator is live, integrate a mock database using Supabase to store user-selected tiers and simulate a lead conversion pipeline.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build high-value business tools that demonstrate real-world engineering capability. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start engineering the infrastructure that powers the modern web. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
